text: The overall injury incidence proportion among runners is approximately 40.2%, with a prevalence proportion of approximately 44.6%.
The knee, ankle, and lower leg are the anatomic locations with the highest proportion of running injury incidence.

Achilles tendinopathy is the most common running injury by incidence proportion at 10.3%, followed by medial tibial stress syndrome (9.4%), patellofemoral pain syndrome (6.3%), plantar fasciitis (6.1%), and ankle sprains (5.8%).
specific_value: Achilles tendinopathy 10.3%, MTSS 9.4%, PFPS 6.3%, plantar fasciitis 6.1%, ankle sprains 5.8%

text: Patellofemoral pain syndrome is the most prevalent running injury at 16.7%, followed by medial tibial stress syndrome (9.1%), plantar fasciitis (7.9%), iliotibial band syndrome (7.9%), and Achilles tendinopathy (6.6%).
specific_value: PFPS 16.7%, MTSS 9.1%, plantar fasciitis 7.9%, ITBS 7.9%, Achilles tendinopathy 6.6%

text: Among ultramarathon runners, the ankle (34.5%), knee (28.1%), and lower leg (12.9%) are the three most frequently injured anatomic sites.
Injury incidence proportions by anatomic location do not significantly differ between ultramarathon and non-ultramarathon runners.
